## engineering.coding.explicit_diagnosable_flow - Explicit Diagnosable Flow

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `should`
- subtype: `coding_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `coding`, `architecture`, `diagnostics`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml`

Prefer explicit data flow, ownership, boundaries, and verification over hidden or clever behavior.

Keep implementation easy to trace mechanically: clear entry points, explicit state changes, source-of-truth ownership, boundary normalization, and verification attached to risky behavior.

Rationale: SocratexPipeline is meant to preserve long-running agent continuity; implicit systems make that continuity fragile.

Sources:
- `core/AGENT-CONTRACT.yaml` `operating_principles` - Consolidated from the shared agent contract.
- `project/code/WORKFLOW.yaml` `general_workflow` duplicate - Code-project workflow repeats the same future-fit and diagnostic bias.
name: engineering.coding.explicit_diagnosable_flow
summary: Prefer explicit data flow, ownership, boundaries, and verification over hidden or clever behavior.
title: Explicit Diagnosable Flow

## engineering.workflow.knowledge_file_fallback - Use File Knowledge Fallback When SQLite Is Unavailable

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `should`
- subtype: `workflow_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `workflow`, `docs-workflow`, `verification`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml`

The JSON table fallback mirrors the core SQLite table model but intentionally omits named views.

If SQLite cannot be used, read compiled knowledge from `AI-compiled/project/knowledge-files/` with `tools/knowledge_file_select.ps1`; query by tags, type, source path, document path, entry name, or startup flag instead of named views.

Rationale: Some hosts may not allow SQLite writes or may lock the database file, but agents still need a compact compiled knowledge layer.

Sources:
- `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml` `tagged_rules` - Source pipeline file-fallback rule.
name: engineering.workflow.knowledge_file_fallback
summary: The JSON table fallback mirrors the core SQLite table model but intentionally omits named views.
title: Use File Knowledge Fallback When SQLite Is Unavailable

compiled_content:
## engineering.workflow.knowledge_hash_gate - Validate Source Hashes Before Trusting SQLite

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `must`
- subtype: `verification_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `verification`, `docs-workflow`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml`

A compiled knowledge database is trustworthy only when its tracked source hashes match current documents.

Run `tools/knowledge_check.ps1` before relying on existing compiled knowledge; if stale documents are reported, refresh them with `tools/knowledge_upsert.ps1 -Path <document>` or rebuild with `tools/knowledge_compile.ps1`.

Rationale: Hash checks prevent agents from reading stale compiled knowledge after source documents change.

Sources:
- `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml` `tagged_rules` - Source pipeline starter rule.
name: engineering.workflow.knowledge_hash_gate
summary: A compiled knowledge database is trustworthy only when its tracked source hashes match current documents.
title: Validate Source Hashes Before Trusting SQLite

## engineering.workflow.knowledge_sqlite_generated_layer - SQLite Knowledge Is Generated

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `must`
- subtype: `workflow_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `workflow`, `docs-workflow`, `verification`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml`

The SQLite knowledge database is a compiled index, not a manually edited source of truth.

Edit source documents first, then refresh `AI-compiled/project/knowledge.sqlite` with `tools/knowledge_compile.ps1` for a full rebuild or `tools/knowledge_upsert.ps1 -Path <document>` for a targeted document update.

Rationale: This preserves readable source documents while giving agents fast tag, type, source, and startup-context queries.

Sources:
- `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml` `tagged_rules` - Source pipeline starter rule.
name: engineering.workflow.knowledge_sqlite_generated_layer
summary: The SQLite knowledge database is a compiled index, not a manually edited source of truth.
title: SQLite Knowledge Is Generated

## engineering.workflow.knowledge_views - Query Context Through Named Views

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `should`
- subtype: `workflow_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `workflow`, `docs-workflow`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml`

Use named knowledge views for common context lanes before broader document reads.

Prefer `tools/knowledge_select.ps1 -View <view_id>` for prepared context lanes such as `session_start`, `debugging`, `performance`, and `docs_workflow`; use tag/type/source queries when the task needs a narrower slice.

Rationale: Named views reduce context cost and make repeated agent startup/query behavior consistent across projects.

Sources:
- `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml` `tagged_rules` - Source pipeline starter rule.
name: engineering.workflow.knowledge_views
summary: Use named knowledge views for common context lanes before broader document reads.
title: Query Context Through Named Views

## engineering.workflow.load_standards_before_code - Load Engineering Standards Before Code Work

- document: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml`
- type: `rule`
- priority: `must`
- subtype: `workflow_rule`
- load_at_start: `true`
- tags: `engineering`, `workflow`, `coding`, `architecture`, `docs-workflow`
- source_of_truth: `context-docs/ENGINEERING.yaml`

Programming work should load compiled engineering, coding, and architecture guidance before source edits.

For implementation, refactor, bugfix, or code review tasks, prefer `tools/knowledge_code_context.ps1` before editing code. If the wrapper is unavailable, load the broad rules slice with `tools/knowledge_select.ps1 -Tags engineering,coding,architecture -Match any -Type rule`, then load task-specific views or tags as needed.

Rationale: Professional code quality depends on keeping ownership, architecture, readability, diagnostics, verification, and project best practices active in context during implementation.

Sources:
- `project/code/WORKFLOW.yaml` `read_order` - Code workflow read-order requirement for programming tasks.
- `project/code/WORKFLOW.yaml` `script_first_execution` duplicate - Script-first workflow repeats the preferred query command.
- `tools/knowledge_code_context.ps1` `script` duplicate - Canonical helper for loading the code-task engineering context slice.
name: engineering.workflow.load_standards_before_code
summary: Programming work should load compiled engineering, coding, and architecture guidance before source edits.
title: Load Engineering Standards Before Code Work
